headline: History of Printing
description: Printing (woodblock: China, ~200 CE) → movable type (Bi Sheng, China 1040 CE; Gutenberg, Germany 1450 CE) → printing press → mass literacy, Reformation, Scientific Revolution. Gutenberg Bible (1455): first major book printed with movable type. Printing was the 'Internet of its day' — democratizing knowledge and transforming society.

citation.name: The Printing Revolution in Early Modern Europe (Elizabeth Eisenstein, 2nd Ed)
